complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the project initialization into setting up the monorepo structure, configuring backend with Express and TypeScript, initializing React frontend with TypeScript template, creating shared types module, setting up Docker compose for development, configuring environment variables, setting up development scripts with hot-reload, and configuring ESLint/Prettier with CI/CD pipeline. Each subtask should be independently executable with clear dependencies.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is a greenfield project setup task. The complexity is moderate (3/10) because it involves standard tooling setup with well-documented procedures. The existing Python script provides OAuth flow reference but the web stack needs to be built from scratch. 8 subtasks are appropriate to cover all aspects of modern full-stack setup.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down OAuth implementation into Passport.js setup with Google strategy, token encryption utilities, Redis session store configuration, authentication middleware, OAuth callback handlers, automatic token refresh mechanism, frontend OAuth flow, httpOnly cookie session management, logout functionality, and comprehensive error handling. Focus on security best practices including token encryption, CSRF protection, and proper session management.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: OAuth implementation is complex (7/10) due to security requirements, token management, refresh logic, and Redis integration. The existing Python code shows basic OAuth but the web implementation requires more sophisticated session management and security. 10 subtasks ensure thorough coverage of authentication concerns.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide the Gmail service into setting up authenticated API client, implementing parallel email fetching with label queries, building message parsing utilities, creating label management functions, implementing read/unread toggle, adding comprehensive error handling with retry logic, and building a caching layer. Each subtask should handle specific Gmail API operations with proper TypeScript typing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Gmail API integration is moderately complex (6/10) due to API peculiarities, message format parsing, and deduplication logic. The existing Python code provides a basic reference but the TypeScript service needs robust error handling and caching. 7 subtasks cover all API operations efficiently.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Design and Implement Email List React Component
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the email list component into TypeScript interface definitions, EmailListItem with conditional styling logic, responsive CSS grid layout, print-specific media queries, EmailList container with data fetching, and empty state handling with date formatting. Focus on clean component architecture and reusability.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is a straightforward React component task (4/10) with clear requirements. The main challenges are conditional styling and print CSS. The PRD specifies exact visual requirements making implementation straightforward. 6 subtasks properly separate concerns between data, presentation, and print styling.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide action buttons implementation into component structure setup, React Query mutations configuration, backend API endpoints creation, loading/disabled states, keyboard accessibility with ARIA, toast notification system, and integration with email list. Each subtask should focus on specific interaction patterns and user feedback mechanisms.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Action buttons involve moderate complexity (5/10) with optimistic updates, mutation handling, and accessibility requirements. The integration between frontend mutations and backend API calls requires careful coordination. 7 subtasks ensure proper coverage of all interaction states and accessibility.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Build Settings Page with OAuth Configuration
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down settings page into accessible modal component creation, connection status display component, test connection functionality implementation, OAuth disconnect/reconnect handlers, and error message display with user guidance. Focus on clear user feedback and connection state management.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Settings page is relatively simple (4/10) as it mainly displays status and triggers OAuth flows already implemented in Task 2. The modal and status display are straightforward React components. 5 subtasks adequately cover the UI and connection management aspects.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide synchronization into SyncService lifecycle management, Gmail history API integration, exponential backoff retry mechanism, WebSocket server setup, WebSocket client with reconnection, Redis state persistence, change processing with deduplication, conflict resolution, sync monitoring and health checks, Gmail API rate limiting, and comprehensive error recovery. Each subtask should handle specific aspects of reliable real-time synchronization.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Real-time sync is the most complex task (9/10) involving WebSockets, Gmail history API, Redis persistence, conflict resolution, and robust error handling. The bi-directional sync with retry logic and rate limiting requires sophisticated implementation. 11 subtasks are necessary to properly handle all edge cases and reliability concerns.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Add Manual Refresh and Auto-refresh Features
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down refresh features into RefreshControl component creation, manual refresh with loading states implementation, auto-refresh timer logic with cleanup, and last refresh timestamp display with relative time formatting. Focus on user control and clear visual feedback.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Refresh functionality is simple (3/10) involving basic React state management and intervals. The requirements are clear and implementation is straightforward using React Query's invalidation. 4 subtasks cover all UI and timing aspects adequately.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide print optimization into comprehensive media query setup, hiding non-printable elements, adding dynamic page headers/footers, ensuring color preservation and page breaks, and cross-browser testing with fixes. Each subtask should focus on specific print rendering concerns.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Print CSS is straightforward (3/10) with well-defined requirements for landscape US Letter format. The main work is CSS rules and testing across browsers. 5 subtasks ensure thorough coverage of print-specific styling and browser compatibility.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Security, Error Handling, and Performance Optimization
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down into Express rate limiting setup, CSRF protection configuration, CSP and security headers, React error boundaries, centralized logging system, virtual scrolling for large lists, Redis caching layer, request batching for Gmail API, Web Vitals performance monitoring, and security testing suite. Each subtask should implement specific security or performance measures with proper testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Security and performance optimization is complex (8/10) requiring multiple layers of protection, monitoring, and optimization techniques. This task touches all layers of the application and requires careful implementation to avoid introducing vulnerabilities. 10 subtasks ensure comprehensive coverage of security, error handling, and performance concerns.
